insights: 80% of rejections (16/20) were wrong_market_price, facsimile_signature, or other—indicating overpriced, fake-signed, or deceptive listings.
Confirmed snipes feature on-card autographs from authenticated modern sets (Topps Tribute, Authentic Images) at 82%+ discount vs. market.
Lot listings (multiple cards, incomplete sets, group lots) rejected 100% of the time—single, specific cards only.
Facsimile & printed signatures (gold editions, merrick mint, made in usa) flagged in 3 rejections; zero confirmed snipes used these terms.
Reprint/novelty language (rainbow foil, print, novelty card) appears only in rejections; confirms real snipes are original issue.
Vintage rookies (1989 Griffey) rejected across Bowman, Fleer, Donruss; market price mismatch suggests saturation.
Confirmed snipes leverage parallel rarity (Green /99, Gold Foil Jumbo) + authenticated brands (Topps Tribute, Authentic Images).
Title-to-specifics mismatch (wrong set, wrong card #, wrong parallel) rejected 100%—listing accuracy is critical signal.
